mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7% signals improving labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 7% show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show no significant correlation with HOUST/RSAFS growth (Pearson r < 0.1) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals strengthening manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlook.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO expansion phases show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% signals central bank policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 2% periods show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < -0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) and rise in housing starts (HOUST) indicates conflicting demand signals, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through market uncertainty.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show no significant negative correlation with GDPC1/HOUST divergences (Pearson r > -0.1) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 6-month moving average signals weakening supply chain activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO contraction phases show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < -0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.
